classes.purpose: Select or prepare an interview that addresses the engagement question
classes.content: Campaign definitions; case and project labels; participant name, role, language, introduction and identifier; purpose, background context, hypotheses, topics, questions, red flags, boundaries, and expiry

classes.purpose: Retrieve and interpret the completed interview
classes.content: Status, completion data, transcript events, participant answers, media metadata, generated review, uncertainties, contradictions, missed opportunities, and follow-up questions

boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Create and operate an adaptive external-participant interview and retrieve its evidence
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: The selected campaign or custom brief, participant and project fields, expiry, participant microphone conversation, transcript events, media metadata, and generated quality review
controls: The user selects the hosted-interview workflow before a participant link is created.
Authentication secrets and bearer URLs are kept out of ordinary command arguments and logs.
The separate hosted-service record states the source-backed access and retention position.

boundaries_beyond_codex.destination: A participant or mailing channel selected by the user
boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Deliver the bearer interview link to its intended participant
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: Participant URL and any invitation text
controls: Creating a link does not authorize Clara to send it.
The workflow sends or publishes the link only after the user explicitly asks for that action.

security_controls.control: Input briefs and output receipts containing bearer material are restricted to local mode 0600.
